top_signals.title: Stocks Slip as Traders Trim Risk Before Weekend: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Stocks fell as traders reduced exposure ahead of the weekend, with a fragile truce in the Middle East keeping geopolitical risk in focus.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Korean Levered ETFs Shaking Markets All Around the World
top_signals.summary: Barclays's Alexander Altmann explains the exploding demand.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Odd Lots: Korea’s Levered ETFs Shake Up Global Markets (Podcast)
top_signals.summary: Retail participation in the stock market is booming. And of course the biggest story in markets is the AI trade, which has created an incredible amount of demand for chips and memory. These two broad themes have come together in the form of leveraged, single-stock ETFs. And while these products are popular in the US, the scale coming out of Korea is enormous. It’s a good week to talk about this intersection, because some of the biggest stories of the week include Samsung’s earnings and SK Hynix’

top_signals.title: Over 70% of accommodations in Japan say they are understaffed amid tourism influx
top_signals.summary: Solving the issue is crucial to developing tourism sustainably as Japan's strategic industry, the government said in a white paper released Friday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
